title: Reduced work time of users

posts.author: PHerrmann86 (Philipp Herrmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

our company reduced the work time to 80% which means users are working only 32 instead of 40 hours per week.
This means the users are working either 4 days per week or 2 full days and 2 half days.
If we just add the 32 hours as capacity per week, it calculates 6.5 hours per day, which is not correct for the workload planning.
Is it possible to define the work time of each user?

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @PHerrmann86,

Good question! Thanks for writing in about this. Talia here for the monday.com team.

Have you already tried going into the workload widget and editing capacity per person? This is what I would recommend doing:

image1380×956 150 KB

I’d also love to know if you’ve already checked out this helpful knowledge base article about the workload widget!

posts.author: PHerrmann86 (Philipp Herrmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Talia,

I know this function, but unfortunately this is not helpful.
Even if I change the capacity per user, it will still decide the hours by 5 work days. This is incorrect. We need to have 2 days of 8 hours capacity and for example two days of 4 hours capacity.
How can I realize this?

Thanks for your help.
